# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account and generates a JSON key file for application authentication. Include the key creation in the template so applications can authenticate immediately. This is for our application services that need GCP API access from outside GCP.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
- name: app-service-account
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: app-svc-external-auth
    displayName: External Application Service Account
    description: Service account for external application GCP API access - keys should be rotated every 90 days
    projectId: {{ env["project"] }}

- name: app-service-account-key
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccounts.key
  properties:
    parent: projects/{{ env["project"] }}/serviceAccounts/app-svc-external-auth@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
    privateKeyType: TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE
    keyAlgorithm: KEY_ALG_RSA_2048
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account

- name: app-service-account-iam-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/monitoring.viewer
    member: serviceAccount:app-svc-external-auth@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account

- name: deny-token-creator-self
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
    member: serviceAccount:app-svc-external-auth@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
    condition:
      title: deny-self-impersonation
      description: Prevent the service account from creating tokens for itself or other accounts
      expression: "false"
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account

outputs:
- name: serviceAccountEmail
  value: $(ref.app-service-account.email)
- name: serviceAccountUniqueId
  value: $(ref.app-service-account.uniqueId)
- name: keyName
  value: $(ref.app-service-account-key.name)
- name: keyValidAfterTime
  value: $(ref.app-service-account-key.validAfterTime)
- name: keyValidBeforeTime
  value: $(ref.app-service-account-key.validBeforeTime)
- name: securityNotes
  value: >
    IMPORTANT SECURITY NOTES:
    1. The private key material is returned ONLY at creation time. Store it immediately in a secrets manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
    2. NEVER store the key in version control, logs, or plaintext files.
    3. Rotate this key every 90 days maximum. Delete old keys promptly.
    4. This service account has ONLY monitoring.viewer role - request additional least-privilege roles as needed through your IAM review process.
    5. Monitor key usage via Cloud Audit Logs and set up alerts for anomalous access patterns.
    6. Consider using Workload Identity Federation instead of service account keys where possible for improved security posture.
